finding: compose_merged_migrations moved to SDK, parameterized on (merged_dir, module_roots); ziee wrapper passes its own roots. Fingerprint proof: sha256(sorted-concat migrations-merged/*.sql) == sha256(sorted-concat source union) == 93b6f632; 91==91 files, name sets identical.

ensure_build_db reproduces the inline block byte-for-behaviour: same admin-connect, CREATE-if-missing, swallowed duplicate_database race, fall-back-to-base on unreachable. Per-worktree db name ziee_build_<key> from verbatim-moved worktree_key → identical.

installation_dir/data_dir .expect() → .clone().unwrap_or_else(default). ziee always fills both via resolve_paths, so its Some(..) wins → default branch never fires for ziee → byte-identical runtime behaviour.

DefaultIdentityResolver.authenticate mirrors ZieeIdentityResolver exactly: MISSING_TOKEN 401, invalid-token 401, USER_NOT_FOUND 401, USER_INACTIVE 403 (inactive user rejected BEFORE any group load). No weaker path than ziee's.

validate_jwt_secret refuses <32 bytes + the placeholder denylist (incl the dev.example.yaml value 'dev-secret-change-in-production-min-32-chars-long'). Same guard set as ziee-auth jwt::try_new — an SDK app can't boot with a known signer.

CORS SECURITY: ERROR downgraded to debug ONLY when server.host is loopback (127.0.0.1/localhost/::1); a public bind still emits the full ERROR. No weakening of the actual CORS layer — only the log level changes.
Noop sinks drop events/sync notifications; they emit nothing, so they cannot leak. Correct posture for an app with no bus/sync yet; auth handlers still fire their events (just unconsumed).

worktree_db.rs deleted from ziee (git rm); all 3 consumers (server build.rs, desktop build.rs, harness_inner.rs) rewired to ziee_build_support::worktree_db. grep: no remaining #[path]…worktree_db include in ziee.

ziee-build-support added as [build-dependencies] (server+desktop) + [dev-dependencies] (server harness). Lean crate (sqlx runtime + tokio, no query! → build-DB-free), so it doesn't drag the framework into the build graph. Cargo.lock unifies sqlx onto the workspace version. cargo check -p ziee / -p ziee-desktop = 0.

New turnkey/noop symbols are ADDITIVE re-exports; they define no aide operations/schemas. ziee mounts auth with the UNCHANGED ZieeIdentityResolver and imports none of the new symbols → OpenAPI unaffected (E8 golden identical both surfaces).

provision_build_db builds its OWN current-thread runtime → must not be called from a #[tokio::main] build.rs. ziee (tokio::main) uses ensure_build_db (async) instead; only a plain app uses provision_build_db. Documented on both.

mount_auth layers extensions on the merged router → applies to routes present at layer time. Doc contract: call after registering own protected routes (or serve the returned router). Matches ziee's global-layer boot. Smoke test finish_api → axum::Router proves wiring.
worktree_root handles both /src-app and \src-app separators (Windows); stable_suffix is FNV-1a (no DefaultHasher randomization) so the key is stable across OS/process. Moved verbatim, unit-tested.
serve's shutdown_signal gates SIGTERM behind cfg(unix) with a cfg(not(unix)) pending() fallback; graceful-with-warning if signal install fails (container/seccomp). Mirrors ziee's own shutdown_signal.
No #[ignore]/.skip/.only added anywhere in the diff. The 5 worktree_db unit tests moved verbatim (not weakened); embedded_pg gained 2 real tests; config gained 5; turnkey 2; noop 2. All pass.

Scrubbed the ziee-only 'Config::resolve_paths' panic string (a symbol absent from the SDK). grep: no ziee-domain symbol name remains in embedded_pg. default_embedded_dir uses ziee_core::app_state::get_app_data_dir (SDK-owned, app-name-configurable).

mount_auth folds all 4 hand-written steps (install 3 extensions, trust flag, session-settings seed, mount both route sets) → CytoAnalyst's ~230 lines + 2 trait impls collapse to ~5 (DefaultIdentityResolver::new + NoopAuthEventSink/NoopAuthSyncSink + mount_auth).

Harness worktree_suffix now sources worktree_db from the dev-dep; suffix derivation byte-identical (verbatim move), so per-worktree test-template DB names are unchanged. No behavioural change to the harness.
